notes: Subject of the critique; hosted Scalability Day event in New York. Bill Gates keynoted.
Microsoft CEO and Chairman; opened Scalability Day with the claim 'Any business of any size can now run its enterprise applications on Microsoft software.'
Author's firm; cited prior NT analyses from 1995 and 1996.
Kastner notes Intel processor performance was given 'scant credit' for NT scalability at the event.
Mentioned as having supported Very Large Memories on Unix/Alpha for over a year before Microsoft claimed the feature.
Mentioned alongside Oracle as having supported VLM on Digital Unix/Alpha before Microsoft. Acquired by IBM in 2001.
Mentioned as platform where Oracle and Informix already supported VLM — Unix on Alpha.
Implied Unix competitor; Aberdeen cited Unix as retaining enterprise high-end. Sun was the leading Unix vendor.
AS/400 mentioned as legitimately retaining enterprise high-end alongside Unix and mainframe.
